messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Gpt 5.5 posts its summary using Task Complete tool -- the summary should be shown in UI ISSUE BODY: repro: 1. ask gpt 5.5 to do something in Agents window using CLI 2. it completes the task and invoked "Task Complete" with the summary of what it's done -- however the summary is not shown in UI leaving the user without summary of what the agent has done Version: 1.125.0-insider (Universal) Commit: 43a13cad7f4d7c1f0c87dd6459bb6d85f394d5fb Date: 2026-06-12T09:57:11-07:00 Electron: 42.2.0 ElectronBuildId: 14159160 Chromium: 148.0.7778.97 Node.js: 24.15.0 V8: 14.8.178.14-electron.0 OS: Darwin arm64 25.5.0 --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- #### 5. Summarize the changes - After successfully upgrading the @github/copilot package and ensuring that all tests are passing, you must create a summary of the changes that were made during the upgrade process. - Give a summary of the changes in the code base - Give a summary of the changes in the tests - Give a summary of the differenes in the type definitions between the old and new versions of the @github/copilot package. - Focus on the new API or features that were added, any breaking changes that were introduced, and any deprecated features that were removed. - Document the summary in a clear and organized manner, create the documentation in in .build/upgrade-notes.md ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\.agents\skills\launch\SKILL.md ===== --- name: launch description: "Launch and automate VS Code Insiders with the Copilot Chat extension using @playwright/cli via Chrome DevTools Protocol. Use when you need to interact with the VS Code UI, automate the chat panel, test the extension UI, or take screenshots. Triggers include 'automate VS Code', 'interact with chat', 'test the UI', 'take a screenshot', 'launch with debugging'." metadata: allowed-tools: Bash(npx @playwright/cli:*) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### 2. Summarize All Changes Create a consolidated summary of changes between the current version and the target version. Group changes by category, not by individual version: **Summary Format:** ```markdown ### `@anthropic-ai/package-name` (oldVersion → newVersion) #### Features - **Category:** Description of new feature or capability #### Bug Fixes - Description of what was fixed #### Breaking Changes - **Old API → New API**: Description of what changed and how to migrate ``` **How to Create the Summary:** 1. **Read the GitHub Release Notes**: Go through each release between your versions 2. **Consolidate by Category**: Group all features together, all bug fixes together, etc. 3. **Identify Breaking Changes**: Look for: - Removed or renamed exports - Changed function signatures - Modified type definitions - Deprecated APIs that have been removed 4. **Document Migration Steps**: For breaking changes, include the old and new patterns 5. **Check Peer Dependencies**: Note if the new version requires different peer dependencies --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Where the agentSdks gating lives Inside `packageTask`'s `jsonEditor` callback (the same one that injects `commit` / `date` / `checksums` / `version`), `readAgentSdkResults()` loads the results file (returns `{}` when the env var is unset) and merges `agentSdks` into `product.json`. The REH gulpfile only writes `agentSdks` for `type === 'reh'`; the REH-web variant skips it because the agent host is node-only and the SDK config has no consumer in a browser-served server. Local `gulp vscode-darwin-arm64` invocations don't set `AGENT_SDK_RESULTS_FILE` and don't have `VSCODE_PUBLISH=true`, so `readAgentSdkResults()` returns `{}` and product.json ships without `agentSdks` — same UX as today's no-config build. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ## Why two steps, not inline-in-gulp The agent SDK work is a distinct concern from the VS Code packaging gulp graph. As its own pipeline step: - Visible in the build log — operators see a discrete "Agent SDK: build + upload" step they can click into instead of grepping inside "Build client" output. - Independently re-triggerable — if the SDK step fails, the operator can re-run just the platform job; if it succeeds but the gulp step fails, the SDK upload is already idempotent (HEAD-then-skip). - Doesn't add async-stream complexity to the gulpfile. `packageTask` stays a sync stream-returning function; the only change is one synchronous `readAgentSdkResults()` call inside the existing `jsonEditor` callback. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\prompts\implement.prompt.md ===== --- agent: agent description: 'Implement the plan' tools: ['edit', 'runNotebooks', 'search', 'new', 'runCommands', 'runTasks', 'usages', 'vscodeAPI', 'problems', 'changes', 'testFailure', 'openSimpleBrowser', 'fetch', 'githubRepo', 'extensions', 'todos', 'runTests'] --- Please write a high quality, general purpose solution. Implement a solution that works correctly for all valid inputs, not just the test cases. Do not hard-code values or create solutions that only work for specific test inputs. Instead, implement the actual logic that solves the problem generally. Focus on understanding the problem requirements and implementing the correct algorithm. Tests are there to verify correctness, not to define the solution. Provide a principled implementation that follows best practices and software design principles. If the task is unreasonable or infeasible, or if any of the tests are incorrect, please tell the user. The solution should be robust, maintainable, and extendable. ===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\.github\prompts\issue-grouping.prompt.md ===== --- agent: agent model: Claude Sonnet 4.5 (copilot) argument-hint: Give an assignee and or a label/labels. Issues with that assignee and label will be fetched and grouped. description: Group similar issues. tools: - github/search_issues - agent/runSubagent - edit/createFile - edit/editFiles - read/readFile ---

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Display Agent Task Summary in UI after Task Complete",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["frontend", "agents", "ui-display", "chat-panel"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a Copilot Agent user, I want to see the task summary displayed in the UI when an agent completes a task using the Task Complete tool, so that I can understand what work was accomplished without having to ask follow-up questions.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given an agent has completed a task and invoked the 'Task Complete' tool with a summary, When the user views the chat/task panel, Then the complete summary text should be displayed in the UI",
    "Given the Task Complete tool returns JSON with summary content, When rendering the task completion view, Then the summary field should be extracted and rendered as formatted text",
    "Given there is no agent task summary available, When displaying a task completion state, Then show an appropriate 'No summary available' message instead of empty UI",
    "Given the user clicks on a task completion entry, When viewing details, Then the full summary should be visible in the expanded view",
    "Given the chat panel shows recent tasks, When scrolling through completed agent tasks, Then summaries from Task Complete tool calls are included"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Identify API endpoint that returns task completion results with summary",
      "Add fallback handling for missing/empty summary in response handler",
      "Ensure proper JSON parsing and error handling for summary field"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Locate the component responsible for rendering task completion summaries (likely ChatPanel or TaskCompletionView)",
      "Update the summary display logic to handle null/undefined values gracefully",
      "Add formatting for long summaries with word wrapping and scrolling if needed",
      "Ensure proper markdown/text formatting is applied to agent-generated summaries"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test cases for Task Complete tool response with valid summary",
      "Test edge case: empty/missing summary field in response",
      "Verify UI renders correctly across different screen sizes",
      "Confirm chat history preserves task completion summaries"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by team member",
    "All unit tests passing for affected components",
    "E2E test created to verify Task Complete summary display",
    "UI renders correctly in all supported browsers",
    "No regressions in existing agent task functionality"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Which specific UI component(s) should display the Task Complete summary? (Chat panel, sidebar tasks view, or both?)",
    "Is there an existing API endpoint that returns task completion results with a 'summary' field?",
    "Are there any known workarounds or temporary fixes for this issue?",
    "Should summaries support markdown formatting or plain text only?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The Task Complete tool is implemented and working correctly on the agent side",
    "Task completion results are being sent to the backend API successfully",
    "The frontend has existing infrastructure for displaying task-related information",
    "This is primarily a display/rendering issue rather than a data transmission problem"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Phase 1: Backend API Response Handling",
      "description": "Add proper handling for summary field in task completion responses with fallbacks"
    },
    {
      "name": "Phase 2: Frontend Display Implementation",
      "description": "Update UI components to render agent task summaries correctly"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task": "Backend API Response Handling", "points": 3, "assignee": "backend-dev"},
    {"task": "Frontend Display Implementation", "points": 5, "assignee": "frontend-dev"}
  ],
  "context_sources": ["vscode_ai_docs_full.md", "SKILL.md"]
}
```
